arguments.task: Open and read the strongest official first-party pages for Twenty CRM that support these fields: one-line product description, API existence and type, documented authentication methods for the API, pricing/access tier or any gating language, API breadth, rate limits if documented, and whether an official MCP server/integration exists. Prefer canonical pages on twenty.com or official docs/github pages linked by Twenty. Do not rely on search snippets. Return the URLs visited and concise extracted facts with quotations where possible.

raw_result.description: Open-source CRM platform with APIs and app extensions for managing people, companies, opportunities, and custom objects.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs indicate API access via API keys and OAuth 2.0 flows. The OAuth docs mention PKCE and client credentials. API keys are mapped to token per schema.
raw_result.gating_reason: Twenty provides public developer documentation and public pricing, and the product is open source/self-serve from its website.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs describe REST and GraphQL APIs, including Core and Metadata APIs for CRUD on records such as people, companies, opportunities, and custom objects, plus schema management.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP integration was verified from the gathered official evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Developer docs gathered in-browser indicate 100 requests per minute and batch size limits of 60 records.
raw_result.notable_signals: Open-source CRM; public developer docs; supports custom apps and both REST and GraphQL APIs.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The no-official-MCP conclusion was based on absence in the gathered pages rather than an explicit official statement, and the exact canonical OAuth page path may differ from the browser-captured path.
